pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - In the Cloud Forest - Video

pageCaption.value: As part of the Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition, Quechua biologist and National Geographic Explorer Ruthmery Pillco Huarcaya is searching for Andean bears that regenerate cloud forests which feed water into the Amazon River basin.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - In the Cloud Forest - Video

metadescription.value: Ruthmery Pillco Huarcaya is revealing how Andean bears could impact the future of the Amazon's cloud forests and river basin. Watch the video on rolex.org.

description.value: The Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition

text.value: In the cloud forests of the Andes, mist bathes the mountains. The dense, luscious vegetation captures this moisture and feeds it back into the ecosystem, eventually forming the streams and rivers that make up the Amazon River basin. Quechua biologist and National Geographic Explorer Ruthmery Pillco Huarcaya has been trekking through the cloud forests in search of Andean bears. As they roam, the bears regenerate the forests by dispersing seeds from the plants they have eaten, protected by other organic material that has passed through their digestive systems. By helping the Andean bears, Pillco Huarcaya hopes to protect and preserve the cloud forests, which are now under threat from deforestation and climate change.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working closely with communities in Bali to protect some of the world’s most biodiverse coral reefs.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Founder of the Coral Triangle Center, Rili Djohani has dedicated her life to protecting Nusa Penida's coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Mount Logan Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Deep within the ice on the summit of Mount Logan, Canada’s highest peak, are clues about the Earth’s climate and atmosphere spanning thousands of years. Scientist and high-altitude mountaineer Alison Criscitiello led the Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition to retrieve these layers of past climates. What they find will help us understand past changing climates, and what they mean for our future.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Mountaineer Alison Criscitiello is expanding ice core research to new latitudes through her expedition to Mount Logan. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

pageCaption.value: With support from Rolex and the National Geographic Society, climate scientists and Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews endured snowstorms and sub-zero temperatures to install a state-of-the-art weather station on the summit of one of Peru’s highest mountains.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

metadescription.value: Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews have successfully installed a weather station in the high mountains of the Andes. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Mamirauá Wetlands - Video

pageCaption.value: What does the future look like for the forests of the Amazon? Julia Tavares, a plant ecologist joins digital ecologist and fellow National Geographic Explorer Thiago Silva on an epic expedition to the heart of the world’s largest rainforest.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Mamirauá Wetlands - Video

metadescription.value: Researchers Thiago Silva and Julia Tavares try to predict upcoming climate changes and save the Amazon wetlands. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Rolex Awards Video

pageCaption.value: Restoring the dwindling biodiversity of the unique montane cloud forests of Sri Lanka through the Agrapatana Montane Forest Restoration Initiative earned conservationist Rohan Pethiyagoda a Rolex Award for Enterprise in 2000.

WaPageTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Video

metadescription.value: Since the Rolex Award in 2000, Rohan Pethiyagoda has stepped up his initiative to restore Sri Lanka's unique ecosystem. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - At the River Mouth - Video

pageCaption.value: National Geographic Explorers Angelo Bernardino and Margaret Owuor have braved storms, mosquitoes and tidal bores to unlock the secrets of one of the world’s least-studied ecosystems: the mangrove forests at the Amazon River mouth.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - At the River Mouth - Video

metadescription.value: Explorers Angelo Bernardino and Margaret Owuor are shining a light on the Amazon River's unexplored mangrove forests. Watch the video on rolex.org.
